what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that records a design argument about annotation threading: it asserts that threads must have a resolve/reopen lifecycle, identifies missing behaviors in the current brief, and proposes mapping resolution state onto the existing observation-thread model.
observations: Title is 'P6 · 5. Threads must RESOLVE, not just accumulate — the open/closed state is ' and appears truncated
Author session is ch-3mpkjg3r, channel dragnet-development, type block, source claude_code, state current
Has a single part_of link to board://item-a72b23c2
Created and updated timestamps are identical, with a one-entry history noting 'filed'
Argues that threading without resolution state is just a chat log, not markup
Identifies missing resolve action and a permission convention: the query author should not resolve their own query; Tim closes the loop
Identifies missing reopen action and the need for resolved marks to recede visually while persisting as audit trail
Cites brief references BD-F, BD-D, A2, A5 without quoting them
Aligns the proposal with the system's existing pattern: state is composed from the observation-thread, never stored
Proposes that 'resolved' is another observation appended to the thread and the open/closed badge is a computed fold
standouts: The permission rule that Tim must resolve his own raised marks, not the agent
The emotional/UX framing of watching the open-count go to zero
The architectural alignment: resolved state derived from the observation-thread rather than stored separately
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses board:// URI addressing for links
Markdown body with a numbered heading and bullet list
References external design-brief items by shorthand (A2/A5, BD-F, BD-D)
concerns: No code symbols; content is a design opinion/prescription rather than an implementation record
Created and updated timestamps are identical, suggesting no subsequent revision
The thread field is empty even though the item is entirely about threading
Cites external brief items without including their text, so the file is not self-contained
notes: A design note arguing that annotation threads need a resolve/reopen lifecycle and that open/closed state should be derived from appended observations rather than stored as a separate field.
questions: Is this item a proposal, a decided rule, or a discussion note?
Who is Tim and what is his exact role/permissions in the system?
What are A2, A5, BD-F, and BD-D in the referenced brief?
Would 'resolved' be a distinct mark type, a property, or a special observation value?
How would the system enforce the rule that an author cannot resolve their own query?
What is the parent item board://item-a72b23c2 and how does this item relate to it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a design argument about thread resolution; the content matches, though it reads more like a persuasive brief than a neutral record.
apparent_themes: Resolution state as a derived fold over an observation-thread
Balancing audit-trail persistence with visual receding of resolved items
Closure loops and ownership of state transitions in collaborative review
Minimal stored state; behavior composed from append-only observations
intention_signals: It is trying to introduce and justify a resolve/reopen lifecycle for annotation threads while showing that the existing observation-thread architecture can absorb it without adding a separate state store.
novelty: The suggestion that 'resolved' is simply another observation appended to the thread, so open/closed status is computed from the thread rather than maintained as stored state.
contribution: It contributes a concrete lifecycle model for annotation threads (resolve, reopen, visual recession) and a derivation rule that folds thread observations into open/closed state, which a fused system could keep as a behavior requirement.
summary_for_embedding: Design noticeboard item arguing annotation threads must have a resolve/reopen lifecycle, with resolved state derived from appended observations rather than stored separately.
intention_for_embedding: To propose that thread resolution be modeled as an observation in the existing observation-thread so open/closed status is computed, not persisted.
